number.title: The Seed of Fire
number.content: One is the number of new beginnings, unity, and the initial spark of creation. It represents the potential of the seed that contains the entire forest within its shell. In the Mayan cosmology, One is the focused intent that precedes all action—the moment the arrow is notched but not yet released. It is a day of singularity, where the energy is concentrated and potent. Experientially, the number One feels like a fresh start or a sudden clarity of purpose. It is the 'I Am' presence, standing alone but connected to the source. The shadow of One can be isolation or a lack of momentum if the will is not engaged. It asks us to define our direction and plant the intention for the cycle ahead with absolute precision.

nawal.title: The Solar Hero
nawal.content: Ajpu is the nawal of the Sun, the blowgunner, and the spiritual warrior. It represents the light of divinity that resides within the human form and the quest for higher consciousness. Ajpu is the archetype of the hero twins from the Popol Vuh who descend into the underworld to defeat the lords of death through wit, art, and resilience. It governs leadership, performance, and the clarity of vision. Ajpu energy is radiant, confident, and triumphant—it is the hunter who aims true. In its shadow, Ajpu can manifest as arrogance, perfectionism, or a need for constant validation. However, its true essence is the humble recognition of one's own divinity and the courage to shine that light into the world.

combined_energy.title: Initiating the Hero's Journey
combined_energy.content: The convergence of the initiating force of One with the solar power of Ajpu marks the beginning of the Hero's Journey. Today is the 'Seed of Divinity.' It is a day to remember who you really are—a spiritual being having a human experience—and to commit to walking your path with nobility and courage. The energy is vibrant, optimistic, and deeply empowering. It asks you to step into the center of your own life and claim your sovereignty. Unlike other beginnings that might be tentative, 1 Ajpu is bold; it is the sun rising over the horizon, chasing away the shadows of doubt. You are being asked to set a high intention for the next 13 days: What mountain are you ready to climb? What truth are you ready to embody? The challenge is to stand in this light without being blinded by your own ego. True heroism is service to the light, not ownership of it. Stand tall, aim high, and let the journey begin.
notes: Set a clear, noble intention for the 13-day cycle.
Stand in the direct sunlight for a few minutes to absorb the solar code.
Identify one area of your life where you have been playing small and decide to step up.
Wear gold or yellow to align with the frequency of the sun.
Meditate on the image of a hunter aiming at a target; what is your target?

birthday.title: The Solar Initiator
birthday.content:
To be born on 1 Ajpu is to be a pioneer of light and a natural-born leader. You possess the concentrated power of the number One combined with the radiant charisma of the Sun. You are a self-starter, often preferring to forge your own path rather than follow others. Your soul carries the archetype of the 'First Hero'—the one who is willing to go first, to take the risk, and to light the way for others. You have a strong sense of personal destiny and a belief that you are here for a specific, higher purpose. People are drawn to your confidence and your innate nobility; you carry yourself with a dignity that commands respect.

Your life path is one of self-discovery and the manifestation of your divine potential. You are likely drawn to the arts, athletics, or leadership roles where you can shine and inspire. However, your journey is not just about external success; it is about the internal victory over fear and darkness. You are a warrior of the spirit, constantly testing your own limits to see how high you can fly.

The challenge for you is the trap of the ego. Because you shine so brightly, you can sometimes become isolated or arrogant, believing you must do everything alone. Your lesson is to realize that true leadership is empowering others to find their own inner sun. You are here to remind the world that divinity is not something to be sought outside, but a flame to be kindled within.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Ajpu (Sun Face/Blowgunner) glowing with internal solar-gold and amber light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 1: a single glowing golden dot. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Mirror of Duality
number.content: Two is the energy of relationship, polarity, and the dynamic tension between opposites. It represents the separation of the One into two—masculine and feminine, day and night, sun and moon. In the Mayan cosmology, Two is the number of the partner and the mirror. It brings choices into focus and asks us to find balance between competing forces. Experientially, a Two day feels like a conversation or a negotiation. It is rarely solitary; it involves the 'other.' The shadow of Two can be indecision, conflict, or seeing separation where there is unity. It teaches us that we define ourselves through our relationships and that duality is the engine of creation.

nawal.title: The Dream of the Waters
nawal.content: Imox is the nawal of the primordial ocean, the collective unconscious, and the dream world. It represents the fluid, chaotic potential from which all form emerges. Imox is the energy of intuition, empathy, and the hidden currents of the psyche. It governs the waters of the earth and the waters of the body (blood, lymph). Imox is the source of artistic inspiration but also of 'divine madness.' In its shadow, Imox can manifest as confusion, emotional overwhelm, or a loss of touch with reality. However, its aligned state is the profound receptivity of the mystic who can hear the whispers of the universe. It is the womb of creation.

combined_energy.title: Reflecting on the Dream
combined_energy.content: When the polarizing mirror of Two meets the watery depths of Imox, we enter a day of 'Reflective Intuition' or 'Dream Partnering.' The solar clarity of the previous day (Ajpu) now plunges into the subconscious ocean. Today is about understanding the dual nature of your own psyche—the rational mind vs. the intuitive feeling. It is a powerful day for relationships, but specifically the emotional and psychic undercurrents within them. You may find that you and a partner are telepathically linked today, or that your dreams provide a direct answer to a waking dilemma. The energy is fluid, sensitive, and permeable. The challenge is that the 'mirror' of the Two can reflect distortions when placed in the rippling waters of Imox. Be careful of projecting your own unhealed emotions onto others. Use the balance of the Two to navigate the waves; don't just float, steer. It is a day to find the harmony between what you see and what you feel.
notes: Share a dream with a partner or friend and explore its meaning together.
Practice 'mirror gazing' meditation to connect with your deeper self.
Be mindful of boundaries; it is easy to absorb others' emotions today.
Create art that explores a duality (light/dark, happy/sad).
Take a bath with sea salt to cleanse your energetic field.

birthday.title: The Intuitive Partner
birthday.content:
To be born on 2 Imox is to be a soul who navigates life through deep feeling and connection. You are the ultimate empath, possessing the watery intuition of Imox balanced by the relational focus of the number Two. You do not experience life alone; you are constantly feeling the emotional temperature of those around you. You have a gift for understanding the hidden dynamics in relationships—what is *not* being said is often louder to you than what is. You are likely a natural healer, counselor, or artist, using your sensitivity to bridge the gap between souls.

Your life path involves learning to trust your intuition as a valid form of intelligence. You may struggle with the logical, rigid structures of society, preferring the fluid world of dreams and feelings. You are a 'mirror' for others, reflecting their deepest truths back to them, often without even trying. This makes you a magnetic but sometimes intense companion.

The challenge for you is maintaining your own center. Because you are so open and focused on the 'other,' you can easily lose yourself in the currents of someone else's life. You may struggle with codependency or emotional overwhelm. Your lesson is to build a strong vessel for your water—to learn where you end and others begin. When you master this boundary, you become a powerful conduit for collective wisdom.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Imox (Water/Crocodile/Chalice) glowing with internal sea-green and deep turquoise light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 2: two glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Rhythm of Breath
number.content: Three is the number of movement, rhythm, and the breaking of stagnation. If One is the point and Two is the line, Three is the triangle that brings stability through motion. It represents the flow of energy, communication, and the creative spark. Three is often associated with the nervous system and the wind. Experientially, a Three day feels active, buzzing, and potentially restless. It is a day where things want to move—ideas, bodies, conversations. The shadow of Three can be scattered energy, anxiety, or a lack of focus. It asks us to express ourselves and to let the energy circulate freely.

nawal.title: The Sacred Breath
nawal.content: Iq' is the nawal of the wind, the breath of life, and the spirit. It represents the invisible force that animates matter. Iq' is the messenger, the carrier of words, and the power of inspiration. It governs the respiratory system and the mind's agility. Iq' energy is quick, purifying, and sometimes unpredictable—like a sudden gust that clears the air. In its shadow, Iq' can manifest as anger, erratic behavior, or empty speech. However, its highest expression is the clear channel of communication between the human and the divine. It is the breath that calms the mind and the wind that spreads the seeds of change.

combined_energy.title: The Winds of Change
combined_energy.content: Today, the rhythmic movement of Three amplifies the windy nature of Iq', creating a day of high-velocity energy and 'Activated Spirit.' This is a double dose of movement and air. The energy is fast, communicative, and inspiring. It is an incredible day for brainstorming, writing, speaking, or traveling. The wind is not just blowing; it has a rhythm, a beat. You might feel a surge of creative ideas or a sudden urge to express something you've been holding back. The universe is speaking loudly today, likely through synchronicities, overheard conversations, or sudden insights. The challenge is the potential for chaos. 3 Iq' can feel like a hurricane if not grounded. Anxiety levels may be high, and words can be sharp or scattered. The key is to breathe. Conscious breathing aligns you with the rhythm of the day, turning the chaotic gale into a steady, propulsive wind. Let the old stale air be blown out of your life to make room for fresh inspiration.
notes: Practice deep, rhythmic breathing or Pranayama exercises.
Write freely in a journal without editing; let the words flow.
Open the windows in your home to physically change the air.
Have that difficult conversation, but keep your tone measured.
Go for a run or a walk to work off the excess nervous energy.

birthday.title: The Voice of the Spirit
birthday.content:
To be born on 3 Iq' is to be a messenger of the winds and a catalyst for movement. You are a person of high vibration, quick wit, and boundless curiosity. The number Three gives you a need for variety and rhythm, while Iq' connects you to the world of ideas and communication. You are likely a gifted speaker, writer, or performer—someone who uses words to shift energy. You cannot stay still for long; your soul is like the breeze, always exploring new territories and perspectives.

Your life path is one of expression and inspiration. You are here to circulate information and energy. You may be the person who connects different groups of people or who brings new, radical ideas into a stagnant system. You have a spiritual sensitivity that allows you to 'hear' the truth in the wind, giving you flashes of insight that others miss.

The challenge for you is focus and grounding. You can be prone to anxiety, inconsistency, or scattering your energy in too many directions. Like a storm, you can be destructive if your power isn't channeled. Your lesson is to learn to harness your breath and your words with intention. When you do, your voice becomes a powerful instrument of healing and transformation.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Iq' (Wind/T-Shape) glowing with internal sky-blue and crystalline white light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 3: three glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Cube of Stability
number.content: Four is the number of stability, foundation, and the physical plane. It represents the four directions (East, West, North, South) and the four elements that uphold the material world. After the movement of Three, Four brings the energy back to earth to be grounded and structured. It is the energy of the cube and the square—solid, reliable, and enduring. Experientially, a Four day feels steady and practical. It is a time to build, organize, and establish boundaries. The shadow of Four can be rigidity or stubbornness. It asks us to create a stable container for our lives.

nawal.title: The Dawn of Possibility
nawal.content: Aq'ab'al is the nawal of the dawn, the twilight, and the threshold between worlds. It represents the moment when the darkness gives way to the light, symbolizing hope, clarity, and new beginnings. Aq'ab'al is the energy of potential—the realization of a new day or a new concept. It governs the polarity of young and old, and the transition states. In its shadow, Aq'ab'al can manifest as duality, confusion, or deception—the trick of the half-light. However, its highest expression is the clear vision of the morning star, bringing the promise of renewal.

combined_energy.title: Grounding the New Dawn
combined_energy.content: When the stabilizing force of Four meets the hopeful dawn of Aq'ab'al, we have a day of 'Manifested Hope' or 'Structural Renewal.' The fleeting inspiration of the previous days is now asking to be built into something real. Today is about taking a new idea (Aq'ab'al) and giving it legs to stand on (Four). It is an excellent day for starting a new routine, laying the foundation for a project, or physically cleaning your space to welcome new energy. The Four stabilizes the transition of Aq'ab'al, making the change feel less scary and more manageable. The energy is calm, optimistic, and industrious. The challenge is to not let the stability turn into stagnation; ensure that the walls you build have windows for the new light to enter. Use this day to anchor your dreams in reality. Don't just hope for a better tomorrow; build the structure that will support it.
notes: Create a physical plan or timeline for a new goal.
Wake up at dawn and stand facing the East to honor the direction of new beginnings.
Declutter your entryway to clear the path for new energy.
Focus on 'foundational' self-care: sleep, hydration, and healthy food.
Meditate on the image of a temple being built at sunrise.

birthday.title: The Architect of Hope
birthday.content:
To be born on 4 Aq'ab'al is to be a builder of new realities. You possess a unique blend of practical stability (Four) and visionary optimism (Aq'ab'al). You are not just a dreamer; you are someone who knows how to make dreams come true through hard work and planning. You likely have a talent for spotting potential—in people, in projects, or in properties—before anyone else does. You are the one who buys the 'fixer-upper' and turns it into a palace.

Your life path involves bridging the gap between what is and what could be. You are comfortable in transition phases, often acting as a guide or stabilizer for others going through change. You have a youthful spirit that never quite ages, yet you carry the wisdom of an old soul. People rely on you because you are steady, but they are inspired by you because you are always looking toward the light.

The challenge for you is a tendency towards duality or hesitation. You may see so much potential that you struggle to commit to one path, or you may become too rigid in your methods. Your lesson is to trust that the dawn will come and to keep building the temple to receive it. You are here to show the world that hope is a solid thing.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Aq'ab'al (Bat/Dawn) glowing with internal sunrise-orange and twilight-indigo light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 4: four glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Hand of Effort
number.content: Five is the number of work, empowerment, and the human hand. It represents the energy of application—using one's will and effort to move things forward. Five is the center point that drives the four directions into action. It is an industrious, net-gathering energy. Experientially, a Five day asks for participation; it is not a day for passivity. It feels busy, driven, and capable. The shadow of Five can be forcing things, overworking, or losing the spiritual connection in the busyness of the material. It reminds us that we have the power to shape our reality.

nawal.title: The Net of Abundance
nawal.content: K'at is the nawal of the net, the web, and the gathering. It represents the energy of abundance that comes from connection and storage. K'at is the lizard that carries the seeds and the mesh bag that holds the corn. It governs the gathering of people, resources, and ideas. However, the net can also represent entanglement—the debts, burdens, and emotional baggage we carry. In its shadow, K'at can manifest as feeling trapped, hoarding, or being unable to let go. Its highest purpose is to help us sort through what we have gathered, keeping what nurtures us and releasing what weighs us down.

combined_energy.title: Working the Net
combined_energy.content: Today, the industrious energy of Five combines with the gathering power of K'at to create a day of 'Active Sorting' or 'Empowered Gathering.' The energy is focused on the work of the net: pulling in resources, connecting with community, and sorting through your catch. It is a powerful day for networking, fundraising, or organizing your physical and digital lives. The Five gives you the drive to deal with the 'mess' that K'at can sometimes bring. You have the power today to untangle knots that have been bothering you for a long time. Whether it's a financial debt, a cluttered closet, or a complicated relationship, put your 'hand' (Five) to the 'net' (K'at) and get to work. The challenge is the potential for overwhelm; the net can feel heavy. Do not try to carry it all. Use the discernment of the day to keep only the golden fish and throw the rest back. Effort applied today yields a rich harvest.
notes: Pay a bill or resolve a lingering financial task to clear your net.
Reach out to five people in your network to reconnect.
Spend 30 minutes decluttering a specific area of your home.
Visualize cutting cords to burdens you no longer want to carry.
Make a list of resources you need for your goals and take action to acquire them.

birthday.title: The Weaver of Abundance
birthday.content:
To be born on 5 K'at is to be a master of resources and connection. You have a natural talent for bringing things and people together. The number Five gives you a strong work ethic and a drive to make things happen, while K'at endows you with a magnetic quality—opportunities, people, and material goods seem to find their way into your net. You are likely the 'connector' in your social circle, the one who knows the guy who knows the guy. You have a potential for great material and spiritual abundance.

Your life path involves learning the balance between holding on and letting go. Because you gather so easily, you can become weighed down by your own success or by the emotional demands of the many people you support. You may struggle with a cluttered life—either physically or mentally. Your lesson is to constantly curate your net, ensuring that what you carry is serving your highest purpose. You are a worker for the collective, weaving a web that supports not just yourself, but your entire community.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for K'at (Net/Lizard) glowing with internal harvest-gold and earthy-green light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 5: a single glowing golden bar. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Flow of Stability
number.content: Six is the number of flow, organic balance, and responsiveness. It represents the point where the initial effort of the 1-5 cycle stabilizes and finds its rhythm. Six is associated with the Heart of the Sky and Heart of the Earth, bringing a vertical alignment. Experientially, a Six day feels smoother and more harmonious. Obstacles tend to dissolve if you stop pushing and start flowing. It is a time for family, physical vitality, and acceptance. The shadow of Six can be passivity or drifting. It teaches us to trust the process and move with the current.

nawal.title: The Serpent of Wisdom
nawal.content: Kan is the nawal of the serpent, the life force (kundalini), and evolution. It represents the inner fire that coils at the base of the spine and rises to bring enlightenment. Kan is the energy of DNA, sexuality, and the power of transformation through shedding skin. It governs the nervous system and physical truth. Kan is an intense, instinctual energy—it is the raw power of nature. In its shadow, Kan can be manipulative, seductive, or prone to misuse of power. However, its highest expression is the wise use of vitality to serve the evolution of consciousness.

combined_energy.title: Flowing with Vitality
combined_energy.content: When the harmonious flow of Six meets the evolutionary power of Kan, we experience a day of 'Rhythmic Vitality' or 'Fluid Power.' The intense energy of the serpent is softened and guided by the flow of the Six, making it a day of graceful strength rather than explosive force. It is an incredible day for physical movement—yoga, dance, or Tai Chi—where you can connect with the wisdom of your body. The energy supports healing, especially of the nervous system. You may feel a sense of alignment between your physical desires and your spiritual path. The 'shedding of skin' happens naturally today, without the usual pain. The challenge is to stay present in your body. The energy is high, and if blocked, it can manifest as anxiety or restlessness. Let it move. Trust the intelligence of your life force; it knows exactly where it needs to go to heal and evolve you.
notes: Do a yoga session focusing on the spine to awaken the Kan energy.
Spend time in nature, specifically observing the movement of water or wind.
Trust your gut instincts; your body is a truth-teller today.
Engage in intimate connection with a partner or yourself.
Drink plenty of water to help the energy flow through your system.

birthday.title: The Graceful Serpent
birthday.content:
To be born on 6 Kan is to be a master of fluid power and physical wisdom. You possess a natural grace and charisma that is undeniable. The number Six gives you a harmonious, responsive nature, smoothing out the often intense edges of the Kan serpent energy. You are likely very comfortable in your body—perhaps an athlete, a dancer, or a healer who works with somatic energy. You have an intuitive understanding of the cycles of life and death, knowing instinctively when it is time to shed a skin and move on.

Your life path is one of embodied evolution. You are here to show that spiritual growth doesn't mean leaving the body, but fully inhabiting it. You may have a strong magnetic pull on others, and your challenge is to use this influence with integrity. You are a natural teacher of balance, demonstrating how to be strong without being rigid, and how to be powerful without being aggressive. You flow through life's changes with a serpentine elegance that inspires everyone around you.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kan (Serpent) glowing with internal emerald-green and magma-red light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 6: one glowing golden bar with one glowing dot above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Summit of Vision
number.content: Seven is the number of the midpoint, the top of the pyramid, and mystical alignment. It represents the ability to see the past (1-6) and the future (8-13) simultaneously. Seven is a number of reflection, choice, and equilibrium. Unlike the static balance of Two or Four, Seven is a dynamic, spiritual balance. It is a day where energy peaks and shifts. Experientially, a Seven day feels like reaching a lookout point—there is clarity, perspective, and a sense of potential. The shadow of Seven can be indecision born of seeing too many options. It asks us to look at the whole picture.

nawal.title: The Transformer
nawal.content: Kame is the nawal of death, rebirth, and the ancestors. It represents the dimensional door between worlds and the only certainty in life: change. Kame is the compost that feeds the flower—the ending that allows for the beginning. It governs spiritual initiation and the facing of fears. Kame is a peaceful, quiet energy, but it commands total respect. In its shadow, Kame can be morbid, fearful, or clinging to the past. Its highest expression is the profound peace of surrender—knowing that death is simply a transformation of energy.

combined_energy.title: The View from the Threshold
combined_energy.content: Today, we stand at the top of the pyramid (Seven) and look through the veil of worlds (Kame). This is a day of 'Mystical Transformation' or 'Ancestral Perspective.' It is the midpoint of the Ajpu trecena, a time to pause and evaluate the hero's journey. What needs to die for you to continue your ascent? The Seven gives you the detachment to see your own cycles of death and rebirth clearly, without fear. The veil between the living and the ancestors is incredibly thin today. It is a powerful day for mediumship, visiting graves, or simply asking your lineage for guidance. You may feel a sense of 'spiritual vertigo'—a realization of how vast and cyclical existence truly is. The challenge is to not get lost in the other side; keep your feet on the ground even as your head touches the sky. Use this day to make a conscious choice about what you are carrying forward into the second half of the cycle and what you are leaving here, at the summit, as an offering to the past.
notes: Light a candle for a loved one who has passed and ask for their blessing.
Reflect on a past version of yourself and thank them for getting you here.
Visit a place of high elevation or a sacred site if possible.
Practice 'dying' to the moment—letting go of each breath fully.
Journal about what 'rebirth' looks like for you in this current chapter.

birthday.title: The Mystic Traveler
birthday.content:
To be born on 7 Kame is to be a bridge between the worlds. You possess a soul that is deeply familiar with the mysteries of life and death. The number Seven gives you a philosophical, reflective nature, while Kame connects you to the ancestral realms and the power of transformation. You are likely an 'old soul' in the truest sense, carrying wisdom that seems to come from beyond your years. You may have a natural talent for divination, healing, or counseling, as you are not afraid of the dark or the unknown.

Your life path is a series of deep internal shifts. You are constantly dying and being reborn, refining your spirit with each cycle. You are a guide for others during their times of crisis, able to offer a perspective that is both compassionate and detached. Your challenge is to stay grounded in the land of the living. You can sometimes drift too far into the mystical or the melancholic. Your lesson is to bring the wisdom of the 'other side' into this one, living fully and joyfully as a testament to the preciousness of life.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kame (Skull/Closed Eyes) glowing with internal bone-white and soft violet light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 7: one glowing golden bar with two glowing dots above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Weaver of Harmony
number.content: Eight is the number of wholeness, justice, and the octave. It represents the completion of a harmonic cycle and the energy of the masculine. Eight is the number of the weaver, bringing together threads to create a pattern. It is considered a lucky and ceremonial number, often used for weddings and day-keeping rituals. Experientially, an Eight day feels balanced, potent, and organized. It is a time of return and reciprocity. The shadow of Eight can be rigidity or a feeling of being caught in a loop. It asks us to weave the threads of our lives with integrity.

nawal.title: The Stag of the Wild
nawal.content: Kej is the nawal of the deer, the four directions, and the natural world. It represents the balance between the wild and the tame, the physical and the spiritual. Kej is the guardian of the forest and the conductor of natural harmony. It governs strength, agility, and the authority of the shaman. Kej energy is noble and sensitive. In its shadow, Kej can be dominating, stubborn, or fearful. Its highest expression is the graceful leadership that respects the laws of nature and protects the sanctity of the earth.

combined_energy.title: Harmonizing with Nature
combined_energy.content: When the harmonic wholeness of Eight meets the natural strength of Kej, we enter a day of 'Ceremonial Nature' or 'Wild Balance.' This is a very powerful day for connecting with the earth and aligning yourself with natural law. The Eight brings a sense of order and ceremony to the wild energy of the deer. It is the perfect day for an outdoor ritual, a hike, or simply sitting under a tree to regain your center. The energy is robust, noble, and stabilizing. It supports leadership that is rooted in service and balance. You may find that conflicts resolve easily today if approached with fairness and a 'wide' perspective. The challenge is to respect the wildness within and without—do not try to over-domesticate yourself or others. Let the deer run free within the sacred container of the Eight. Find the harmony between your civilized self and your animal soul.
notes: Perform a ceremony in nature (even just pouring water on the earth).
Stand barefoot on the grass to ground your electromagnetic field.
Meditate on the four directions: East, West, North, South.
Practice 'noble silence' for an hour to listen to the world around you.
Eat plant-based foods to honor the deer's diet.

birthday.title: The Guardian of Harmony
birthday.content:
To be born on 8 Kej is to be a pillar of the community and a protector of the sacred balance. You possess a strength that is both gentle and unyielding. The number Eight gives you a love for harmony, ritual, and justice, while Kej connects you to the deep wisdom of the natural world. You are likely a natural leader, perhaps in a spiritual or environmental context. People respect you because you walk your talk; you have an integrity that is felt rather than announced. You are the 'shaman-priest' archetype, capable of mediating between the human world and the wild.

Your life path is one of maintaining equilibrium. You are sensitive to dissonance—in music, in relationships, or in the environment—and you have a drive to restore harmony. You may be drawn to healing arts, ecology, or the arts. Your challenge is to not become too rigid in your definition of 'right.' The deer must be free to move. Your lesson is to lead with the heart and to remember that true authority comes from serving the whole circle of life.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kej (Deer/Hand) glowing with internal forest-green and earth-brown light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 8: one glowing golden bar with three glowing dots above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Circle of Gestation
number.content: Nine is the number of patience, the feminine, and the completion of cycles. It is associated with the nine months of pregnancy and the nine lords of the night. Nine represents the deep, subterranean work that happens in the dark before manifestation. It is an emotional, intuitive number. Experientially, a Nine day feels slower and more internal. It is a time for nurturing, planning, and waiting. The shadow of Nine can be moodiness, withdrawal, or getting stuck in the past. It asks us to trust the timing of the universe.

nawal.title: The Golden Seed
nawal.content: Q'anil is the nawal of the seed, the rabbit, and the star. It represents the spark of life, fertility, and the potential for organic growth. Q'anil is the energy of the planter and the harvester—the promise that what you plant, you will reap. It is a joyful, abundant energy that governs biological life and artistic creation. In its shadow, Q'anil can manifest as impatience, vanity, or problems with fertility. Its highest expression is the blooming garden—the realization of potential into beautiful form.

combined_energy.title: Gestating the Star
combined_energy.content: When the feminine depth of Nine meets the fertile seed of Q'anil, we enter a day of 'Deep Gestation' or 'Nurtured Potential.' This is a profoundly creative and fertile day. The seed (Q'anil) is being held in the womb of the Nine, growing in the dark, safe and warm. It is not a day to force results or to pull the plant up to check the roots. It is a day to water, to sing to, and to trust your creations. Whether you are birthing a child, a project, or a new version of yourself, 9 Q'anil asks for patience and tender care. The energy is soft, golden, and heavy with promise. It is an excellent day for working on long-term projects or for healing reproductive/creative blocks. The challenge is the wait. Q'anil wants to bloom, but Nine says 'not yet.' Trust the timing. The longer the gestation, the more robust the life that emerges.
notes: Spend time nurturing a plant or a garden.
Work on a creative project in private; don't share it yet.
Connect with feminine energy or a mother figure.
Practice patience in a queue or traffic; see it as time given, not lost.
Visualize your biggest dream as a glowing seed in your belly.

birthday.title: The Nurturer of Dreams
birthday.content:
To be born on 9 Q'anil is to be a cosmic gardener with a touch of magic. You possess a deep, nurturing spirit and a profound connection to the cycles of life. The number Nine gives you patience and intuition, while Q'anil fills you with creative potential and a love for beauty. You are likely the person who helps others grow—a teacher, a parent, a mentor, or an artist whose work feeds the soul. You understand that everything has its season, and you are rarely in a rush.

Your life path is one of bringing things to fruition. You may have a 'green thumb' in all areas of life—plants, businesses, and relationships seem to thrive under your care. You have a quiet, magnetic abundance. However, you can sometimes be overly protective or hesitant to let your creations go out into the world. Your lesson is to trust that you have prepared them well. You are here to populate the world with beauty and to remind us that life is a miracle worth waiting for.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Q'anil (Rabbit/Star/Seed) glowing with internal harvest-gold and earthy-green light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 9: one glowing golden bar with four glowing dots above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Hand of Manifestation
number.content: Ten is the number of manifestation, responsibility, and community agreement. It represents the two hands clasping together—collaboration and results. Ten is where the intention of One and the work of Five come to fruition in the physical world. It is a solid, authoritative number. Experientially, a Ten day feels accomplished and 'real.' It is a day where things land. The shadow of Ten can be the burden of responsibility or judgment. It asks us to own our creations.

nawal.title: The Sacred Fire
nawal.content: Toj is the nawal of offering, payment, and the sacred fire. It represents the law of cause and effect (karma) and the need for balance through giving back. Toj is the energy of gratitude expressed through action. It governs the payment of spiritual and physical debts. In its shadow, Toj can manifest as suffering, sickness (the body demanding payment), or a victim mentality. Its highest expression is the joy of service—giving freely to the fire of life to maintain the equilibrium of the cosmos.

combined_energy.title: Manifesting Gratitude
combined_energy.content: When the manifesting power of Ten meets the sacrificial fire of Toj, we have a day of 'Community Offering' or 'Tangible Gratitude.' This is a very strong day for settling debts—financial, energetic, or emotional. The Ten brings the capacity to actually pay what is owed and to see the results of that clearing. It is also a day where your service to others manifests visible rewards. If you have been giving without expectation, 10 Toj is often when the universe balances the books in your favor. The energy is communal and warm. It is a perfect day for a group ceremony, a charity event, or simply buying lunch for a friend. The challenge is to give from a place of power (Ten) rather than obligation. Do not martyr yourself. True offering strengthens the giver as much as the receiver. Make your life an offering today, and watch how the world responds with abundance.
notes: Make a donation to a cause you believe in.
Pay a bill with a feeling of gratitude for the service received.
Light a fire or a candle and offer tobacco, sugar, or prayers.
Do a favor for a neighbor or colleague without being asked.
Reflect on what you are 'indebted' to (ancestors, earth) and say thank you.

birthday.title: The Community Servant
birthday.content:
To be born on 10 Toj is to be a pillar of generosity and a keeper of the balance. You have a deep sense of responsibility towards your community and a natural understanding of the law of reciprocity. The number Ten gives you the ability to organize and manifest, while Toj fills you with a desire to serve and to give back. You are likely the first to volunteer, the one who organizes the fundraiser, or the person who quietly takes care of the family's needs. You understand that true wealth circulates.

Your life path is one of clearing karma—for yourself and your lineage. You may face challenges that feel like 'payments,' but your resilience turns them into offerings. You are a spiritual banker, instinctively knowing how to balance the energetic books. Your challenge is burnout. You can give until you are empty if you aren't careful. Your lesson is to learn that receiving is also part of the cycle. When you allow yourself to be supported, you become an even more powerful vessel for the sacred fire.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Toj (Fire/Offering) glowing with internal ember-orange and smoky-grey light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 10: two glowing golden bars arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Force of Dissolution
number.content: Eleven is the number of dissonance, change, and the breaking of patterns. It is the energy that disrupts the status quo to clear the way for higher order. Eleven is often a challenge—it tests the structures we have built to see if they are truly sound. Experientially, an Eleven day can feel chaotic, surprising, or liberating. It is the loose brick in the wall. The shadow of Eleven is overwhelm or destruction without purpose. It asks us to be flexible and to find the freedom in letting things fall apart.

nawal.title: The Dog of Justice
nawal.content: Tz'i' is the nawal of law, authority, and justice. It represents the dog, the guide, and the faithful companion. Tz'i' governs the natural order and the laws of the universe, as well as human legal systems. It is an energy of loyalty, protection, and unconditional love. However, Tz'i' also has a carnal side—it can represent instinct, desire, and the temptation to stray from the path. In its shadow, Tz'i' can manifest as judgment, betrayal, or legal trouble. Its highest expression is the 'Secretary of God'—the one who records the truth with absolute fidelity.

combined_energy.title: Refining the Law
combined_energy.content: Today, the disruptive force of Eleven meets the order-seeking energy of Tz'i'. This creates a day of 'Correction' or 'Karmic Adjustment.' The Eleven comes to shake up our ideas of justice and truth. It is a day where hidden things may come to light, or where rigid rules are broken to serve a higher law. You might feel a conflict between your instincts and your morals, or between what you want to do and what you 'should' do. The energy is testing your integrity. It is not a comfortable day for those who are hiding something, but it is a liberating day for those seeking truth. Old contracts (Tz'i') may be dissolved (Eleven) to make way for new, more authentic agreements. The challenge is to navigate the chaos without losing your moral compass. Trust that the disruption is actually a course correction. Let the Eleven strip away the hypocrisy so that the true justice of Tz'i' can shine.
notes: Review your own 'code of ethics'; does it still fit who you are?
Be careful with legal documents; read the fine print.
Practice forgiveness for a betrayal (yours or someone else's).
Spend time with a dog; observe its loyalty and presence.
If a plan falls apart today, ask: 'What higher law is intervening here?'

birthday.title: The Challenger of Systems
birthday.content:
To be born on 11 Tz'i' is to be a revolutionary for truth. You possess a sharp mind and a deep sense of justice, but you are not content with the status quo. The number Eleven makes you a change-agent, someone who sees the flaws in the system and wants to fix them, often by breaking them first. Tz'i' gives you a strong moral compass and a loyalty to your cause. You are likely an advocate, a lawyer, a reformer, or a critic—someone who barks when they see injustice.

Your life path is one of refining your own integrity. You may face many tests of character, situations where you must choose between the easy path and the right path. You have a complex relationship with authority; you respect true leadership but rebel against tyranny. Your challenge is to temper your revolutionary zeal with compassion. You can be harsh in your judgments. Your lesson is to learn that the highest law is love, and that true justice restores rather than destroys.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Tz'i' (Dog/Staff) glowing with internal white and judicial-blue light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 11: two glowing golden bars with one glowing dot above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Crystal of Understanding
number.content: Twelve is the number of understanding, summation, and the gathering of scattered parts into a whole. It represents the family, the tribe, and the complex stability that comes after the disruption of Eleven. Twelve is the 'council' number—it holds space for all previous experiences to be integrated. Experientially, a Twelve day feels rich, cooperative, and reflective. It is a time to look back at the journey and see the lesson. The shadow of Twelve can be heaviness or difficulty managing too many moving parts. It asks us to find the unity in diversity.

nawal.title: The Weaver of Time
nawal.content: B'atz' is the nawal of the thread, the monkey, and the artist. It represents the beginning and the creative process itself. B'atz' is the weaver who spins the thread of time and reality. It governs the arts, marriage, and the weaving of destiny. B'atz' energy is playful, clever, and masterfully creative. It is the spark that starts the fire. In its shadow, B'atz' can manifest as superficiality, inability to finish what is started, or getting tangled in one's own threads. Its highest expression is the Master Weaver who creates beauty and connection out of the raw material of life.

combined_energy.title: Weaving the Whole Picture
combined_energy.content: When the integrative power of Twelve meets the creative weaving of B'atz', we have a day of 'Masterful Integration' or 'The Great Tapestry.' Today, the scattered threads of the past 11 days are gathered into a coherent, beautiful design. The Twelve brings the understanding of how everything fits together, while B'atz' provides the skill to weave it. It is a wonderful day for artistic completion, family gatherings, or understanding the 'story arc' of your life. The monkey energy adds a touch of playfulness to the heavy Twelve, making it a day of joyful collaboration. You may see the 'red thread' that connects your diverse experiences. The challenge is complexity—there is a lot going on today. Do not get overwhelmed by the details. Step back and look at the pattern. Trust that every knot and every color has its place in the masterpiece you are creating.
notes: Engage in a craft: knitting, painting, writing, or cooking.
Look at old photos or journals to see the patterns in your life.
Connect with your 'tribe' or family for a shared activity.
Visualize your life as a tapestry; what color thread do you want to add next?
Laugh! B'atz' loves humor as a form of wisdom.

birthday.title: The Master Weaver
birthday.content:
To be born on 12 B'atz' is to be a visionary artist and a unifier of people. You possess a dazzling creativity backed by a deep understanding of human complexity. The number Twelve gives you the ability to hold many different strands of reality at once, while B'atz' gives you the talent to weave them into something beautiful. You are likely the 'glue' in your family or community, the one who remembers the history and tells the stories. You have a playful, charming nature that draws people in, but beneath the humor lies a profound wisdom.

Your life path is one of creative synthesis. You are here to show how things connect—how the past relates to the future, how science relates to art, how you relate to me. You may be a historian, an artist, a community organizer, or a storyteller. Your challenge is focus; you have so many threads in your hands that you can sometimes get tangled. Your lesson is to trust your own design and to keep weaving, knowing that your life is a work of art in progress.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for B'atz' (Monkey/Thread) glowing with internal creative-magenta and rich gold light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 12: two glowing golden bars with two glowing dots above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Light of Ascension
number.content: Thirteen is the number of completion, ascension, and the spirit world. It represents the final step in the trecena cycle—the return to source at a higher level. Thirteen is the number of the ancestors and the 'invisible army' of support. Experientially, a Thirteen day feels mystical, thin-veiled, and sometimes surreal. It is a day of wrapping up and letting go. The shadow of Thirteen can be ungroundedness. It asks us to celebrate the journey and harvest the wisdom.

nawal.title: The Path of Destiny
nawal.content: E' is the nawal of the path, the road, and the journey. It represents the tooth (wisdom) and the destination. E' is the energy of the traveler, the explorer, and the guide. It governs the physical road as well as the spiritual path (the Dao). E' energy is restless, curious, and forward-moving. In its shadow, E' can manifest as aimless wandering, inability to commit, or getting lost. Its highest expression is the conscious pilgrim who walks the path with purpose, knowing that the journey itself is the destination.

combined_energy.title: Ascending the Path
combined_energy.content: We reach the culmination of the Ajpu trecena with 13 E'—the 'Ascended Path' or the 'Spiritual Journey.' The hero's journey that began on 1 Ajpu now completes its cycle. We have walked the road, faced the challenges, and now we stand at the threshold of a new level. Today is a day of profound realization about your life's direction. You may feel a strong connection to your destiny—a sense that you are exactly where you are meant to be, even if the road has been rocky. The Thirteen brings the ancestors close to walk beside you. It is a perfect day for a pilgrimage, a long walk, or a meditation on your future path. The energy is light, expansive, and full of movement. The challenge is to not rush into the next thing too quickly. Pause at this milestone. Look back at how far you have come. Honor the road under your feet. You are not just walking; you are ascending.
notes: Take a long, conscious walk without a destination.
Visualize your life path as a golden road stretching into the stars.
Thank your feet for carrying you this far; give them a massage.
Ask your ancestors for a sign regarding your next step.
Write a summary of the 'hero's journey' you have lived these last 13 days.

birthday.title: The Cosmic Traveler
birthday.content:
To be born on 13 E' is to be a pilgrim of the stars. You possess a restless, adventurous spirit that is always seeking the next horizon. The number Thirteen connects you to the spirit world and the ancestors, making your journey feel guided by invisible hands. E' makes you a natural explorer—of lands, of ideas, and of consciousness. You are likely someone who cannot stay in one place (physically or mentally) for too long; you need movement to feel alive. You are a guide for others, helping them find their way when they are lost.

Your life path is the path itself. You are not result-oriented; you are experience-oriented. You gather wisdom through your travels and trials. Your challenge is grounding. You can be so focused on the 'there' that you miss the 'here.' You may also struggle with commitment. Your lesson is to realize that the ultimate destination is within. When you find your internal compass, your external wandering becomes a sacred pilgrimage, and every step becomes a prayer.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for E' (Path/Tooth/Head) glowing with internal road-dust-white and sky-blue light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 13: two glowing golden bars with three glowing dots above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.
